claim: Keep code files under 500 lines and start fresh conversations frequently to prevent LLM hallucination and degraded outputs
actionable: Split large code files into modules under 500 lines and reset conversations when context gets unwieldy to maintain code quality.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Break work into single feature/task requests per prompt to get better results than asking for multiple things simultaneously
actionable: When prompting AI coding assistants, implement one new feature per prompt and wait for completion before asking for the next.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Use higher-level markdown documents (planning.md, tasks.md) to give context to LLMs and manage project state throughout development
actionable: Create planning.md with architecture/constraints and tasks.md to track TODOs, then reference them in AI conversations.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Always request AI to write tests after each new feature implementation to ensure consistent, high-quality output
actionable: Include test writing as a mandatory follow-up to every feature implementation to maintain code reliability.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Be specific with AI requests including technology details, library names, output format, and exact deliverables rather than vague descriptions
actionable: Provide detailed specifications for AI-generated code including framework choices, output format, and exact requirements instead of high-level descriptions.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Use global rules (system prompts) in AI IDEs to define project-wide guidelines so you don't have to repeat them in every prompt
actionable: Set up workspace-level global rules in your AI IDE (Windsurf/Cursor) to codify all golden rules and project conventions.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Never trust LLMs to manage API keys, database security, and environment variables; always handle security yourself
actionable: Implement all security-related configurations (API keys, database access control, PII handling) yourself rather than delegating to AI.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Set up MCP servers (file system, Brave search, Git) to extend AI IDE capabilities and provide access to documentation and version control
actionable: Configure MCP servers for file system access, web search, and Git to give AI coding assistants access to external tools and information.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Make git commits early and often as checkpoints to recover from broken states, preventing situations where reverting becomes impossible
actionable: Create git commits after each successful feature completion so you can revert to working states without losing progress.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)

claim: Provide documentation examples from GitHub repos or use web search to supply reference implementations that guide AI code generation
actionable: Include links to GitHub examples and request web documentation search in initial AI prompts to provide reference implementations.
source_title: Code 100x Faster with AI, Here's How (No Hype, FULL Process)
